<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Coverage breadth is strong — 13+ files span every significant CLI entry point, providing confidence that refactors don't silently break the CLI contract.
- Zero coupling to other zones confirms tests treat the CLI as a black box (spawn + assert), which is the correct approach for e2e; any test that imports library code should be moved to integration tests.
- With 13+ files and no visible shared fixtures module, watch for duplicated test setup (temp dirs, spawn helpers); a single e2e-helpers file would reduce maintenance burden as the suite grows.
- Thirteen e2e tests with no cross-zone imports confirms correct black-box testing discipline at this layer.
- As the suite grows, extract shared spawn/fixture helpers into a single e2e support module to avoid setup code duplication across test files.
- The e2e zone is the second-largest isolated zone (13 files, coupling 0) yet has no shared fixtures module visible in the file list — at 13+ files, test setup duplication is nearly certain and a single e2e-support.ts would reduce the maintenance surface without breaking the black-box boundary.
- The perfect isolation of both high-cohesion zones (notion-sync-client and rex-cli-e2e-tests) is limited to test-heavy contexts — neither a source-code zone with cohesion above 0.65 nor a source zone with coupling below 0.3 exists in this package, revealing a structural gap: the discipline that produces clean test zones has not been applied to the production source organization.
- At 13 files with coupling 0 and no shared support module, duplicated spawn/fixture setup is the highest-probability maintenance debt in this zone; extracting a single e2e-support file would preserve zero coupling while eliminating copy-paste drift.
- E2e tests across 13 files likely mix behavioral assertions (does the command exit 0?) with output-format assertions (does the JSON match this schema?). A CLI output format change (field rename, table reorder) would invalidate structural assertions across all 13 files simultaneously, conflating format breakage with behavioral regression — separating contract tests from snapshot/format tests would reduce that blast radius.
- E2e suite likely conflates behavioral and output-format assertions. Output format changes will require touching all 13 test files even when behavior is unchanged. Separate behavioral contract tests from output-format snapshot tests to scope change impact.
- The zone ID 'rex-cli-e2e-tests' is package-prefixed ('rex-') despite living inside the rex package — this is redundant and inconsistent with all other zone IDs in the package which omit the package prefix (unit, core, cli, store). The prefix adds noise without disambiguation value since all zones in this package belong to rex.
- The e2e suite includes 'cli-adapter.test.ts' — adapter contract verification is an integration-level concern, not a black-box CLI concern. An e2e test that validates adapter behavior is testing an internal seam rather than an external contract, breaking the black-box discipline that gives the other 12 test files their zero-coupling score.
- 'cli-adapter.test.ts' tests an internal adapter seam at the e2e layer, violating black-box discipline. Move adapter contract verification to the integration test tier where it can import the adapter interface directly without compromising the e2e zone's zero-coupling invariant.
- Zone ID uses redundant 'rex-' prefix inconsistent with all other zone IDs in the package (unit, core, cli, store). Rename to 'e2e' to match the naming pattern of peer zones and reduce ID verbosity.
